patterns.title: 1. First Depositor / Inflation Attacks
patterns.rootCause:
LP tokens are calculated as:

```

liquidity = sqrt(amount0 * amount1)                    // First deposit

liquidity = min(amount0 * totalSupply / reserve0,      // Subsequent deposits

patterns.title: 2. Slippage Protection Vulnerabilities
patterns.rootCause:
Functions that swap tokens or add/remove liquidity don't validate that the received amount meets user expectations. This occurs when:

- `amountOutMin` is set to 0 or not enforced

- No oracle price c

patterns.title: 3. Sandwich & MEV Attacks
patterns.rootCause: Withdrawal swaps without protection

patterns.title: 4. Spot Price Manipulation (slot0)
patterns.rootCause:
`slot0` returns the current tick and sqrtPriceX96 which reflect the most recent swap. An attacker can:

1. Flash loan large amounts

2. Swap to move price

3. Exploit the manipulated price in victim p

patterns.title: 5. Deadline Vulnerabilities
patterns.rootCause: No deadline - transaction can execute anytime

patterns.title: 6. Reserve Manipulation Attacks
patterns.rootCause: IL calculation uses manipulatable reserves

patterns.title: 7. LP Token Calculation Issues
patterns.rootCause: Assumes 18 decimals for all tokens

patterns.title: 8. Callback & Reentrancy Attacks
patterns.rootCause: No access control on callback

patterns.title: 9. Factory & Pool Creation Attacks
patterns.rootCause: Deterministic token address

patterns.title: 10. Decimal & Math Calculation Issues
patterns.rootCause: Assumes both tokens have same decimals

patterns.title: 11. Liquidity Migration & Protocol Upgrade Attacks
patterns.rootCause: Uses raw balanceOf for calculations

patterns.title: 12. Flash Loan-Based Graduation/Threshold Manipulation
patterns.rootCause: Atomic unwrap allows flash loan closure

patterns.title: 13. Imbalanced Liquidity Addition Exploitation
patterns.rootCause:
LP token calculation uses the minimum of two ratios:

```solidity

liquidity = min(amount0 * totalSupply / reserve0, amount1 * totalSupply / reserve1)

```



If tokens are provided in different propo

patterns.title: 14. Rebasing Token Integration Issues
patterns.rootCause: When depositing rebasing tokens, the initial balance equals the deposit amount. If this static balance is cached, it doesn't reflect interest accrual, while the actual token balance continues to grow.

patterns.title: 15. Impermanent Loss Protection Abuse
patterns.rootCause: IL protection calculates losses by comparing initial deposit ratios to withdrawal ratios. If an attacker controls a synthetic token, they can manipulate the price to maximize the calculated IL and dra

patterns.title: 16. Protocol Invariant Breaking
patterns.rootCause: Rounding errors accumulate to break invariant

patterns.title: 17. DEX Swap Slippage Accounting Discrepancies
patterns.rootCause: Uses expected amount, not actual received

patterns.title: 18. Leftover Token Accumulation in Autocompounding
patterns.rootCause: Doesn't consider existing balance before swap

patterns.title: 19. WETH/ETH Pool Asset Mismatch
patterns.rootCause: Pool uses ETH but vault holds WETH

patterns.title: 20. Single-Sided vs Proportional Exit Confusion
patterns.rootCause: Uses single-sided exit during emergency

patterns.title: 21. Type Casting & Integer Overflow Issues
patterns.rootCause:
When casting from larger integer types to smaller ones without validation:

- `u64::MAX` cast to `i64` produces a negative number

- `uint256` values exceeding type limits silently truncate

- Loop co

patterns.title: 22. Spot Price Abuse in Protocol Swaps
patterns.rootCause:
Protocol contracts that:

1. Calculate swap amounts using current DEX spot price

2. Execute swaps without comparing to oracle price

3. Don't have slippage protection on protocol-initiated swaps

patterns.title: 23. LP Token Burn/Hijack Attacks
patterns.rootCause:
1. Public `burn()` function without withdrawal logic

2. Sending burn message to master contract instead of wallet

3. No minimum supply enforcement after burns

patterns.title: 24. Constant Sum AMM Arbitrage
patterns.rootCause:
Constant sum invariant: `k = a + b`



Unlike x*y=k (constant product), the price doesn't change with reserves. If external markets show even slight price deviation:

- Buy the relatively cheaper toke

patterns.title: 25. State Accounting Discrepancies
patterns.rootCause:
State variables are updated incorrectly:

- Subtraction from wrong counter (e.g., reducing `minted` instead of increasing `removed`)

- Parameter misordering in function calls

- Missing state updates

patterns.title: 26. Incorrect Liquidity Ownership Assumptions
patterns.rootCause:
Protocol calculates:

```

lpToRemove = (tokenToRemove * totalLpSupply) / totalReserve

```



But if protocol only owns a fraction of the pool, `lpToRemove` exceeds protocol's LP balance.

patterns.title: 27. Dividend/Reward Gaming
patterns.rootCause:
Dividend calculation uses short-term fee averages:

- Only last N trades (e.g., 20) are considered

- Zero-fee trades can reset the average to 0

- Large dividend allocated relative to manipulated bas

patterns.title: 28. Missing Account Validation (Solana-Specific)
patterns.rootCause:
Solana accounts are passed as parameters; the program must verify:

- Account derivation (PDA seeds)

- Account ownership

- Token mint matches expected

patterns.title: DeFiHackLabs AMM Calculation Errors Compendium
patterns.rootCause: Fee uses 10000 base but K-check still uses 1000 base
